title: Galvanic Cell Visualizer
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: AP Chemistry
description: Animated galvanic cell diagram with salt-bridge ions, electron flow, and Nernst-calculated potentials tied to live cell notation.

learningObjectives: Identify galvanic cell components and explain electron/ion flow directions.
Connect cell notation and Nernst equation calculations to observed cell spontaneity.

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Interactive diagram with linked quantitative readouts
keyQuestions: Which electrode acts as the anode/cathode for a given cell notation?
How do concentration changes shift the cell potential via the Nernst equation?
commonMisconceptions: Electrons travel through the salt bridge rather than the wire.
Both half-cells must have equal ion concentrations for current to flow.
assessmentOpportunities: Students capture the displayed notation and write the matching half-reactions.
Learners adjust concentrations until the cell becomes non-spontaneous and explain why.
